name: Marbled Truffles
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt white chocolate with 1/4 cup of the whipping cream and vanilla extract in a heavy, medium saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly. Pour into a 9-inch square pan and refrigerate.
2. Melt semisweet chocolate chips with butter and remaining 1/4 cup whipping cream in a heavy, medium saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly. Whisk in orange liqueur.
3. Pour the chocolate mixture over the refrigerated white chocolate mixture. Refrigerate until the mixture is fudgy but soft, about 1 hour.
4. Shape about 1 tablespoonful of the mixture into 1 1/4 inch balls. Roll the mixture in your palms to form balls. Place the balls on waxed paper.
5. Sift confectioner's sugar into a shallow bowl.
6. Roll the balls in powdered sugar, placing them in petit four or candy cases. If the coating doesn't stick because the truffle has set, roll between your palms until the outside is soft.
7. Truffles can be refrigerated for 2-3 days or frozen several weeks. Makes about 30 truffles.
